s1.meta: The 45-day sprint · Solo or small-team service businesses
s1.bodyLine1: The sales call tells you where it broke.
s1.bodyLine2: In the offer. In the positioning. In the outreach.
s1.bodyLine4: Every sales call teaches the next one.

s2.bodyLine1: Prospects get on calls. The call feels great.
s2.bodyLine2: Then they say "I need to think about it" or "send me a proposal," and disappear.
s2.bodyLine3: Most founders read that as a closing problem. So they go looking for more leads. Or better scripts.
s2.bodyLine4: None of it works. Because the deal didn't break on the call.
s2.bodyLine5: It broke before it. And every call you take is telling you where.

s3.headline: The work sits in three places. Each one feeds the next.

pillars.heading: What you offer
bodies: You have multiple offers, but your buyer needs one.
Yours gets rebuilt from your sales calls. The deals that closed. The ones that didn't.
They tell us what your buyer needs to hear.
One real buyer. One real problem. The rest follows.
items: Rebuilt from won and lost sales calls, not invented in a strategy doc.
One real buyer, one real problem. Not a workshopped persona.
The foundation everything else runs from. Content, outreach, sales calls.

pillars.heading: What you say
bodies: Maybe you're posting, maybe you're DMing. Either way, it's not landing.
Most of it was written months apart, on different days, in different moods.
Nothing points at the same buyer twice.
Yours all say the same thing now. Profile, site, posts, DMs, the call.
items: Written from your sales calls, in the words buyers actually used.
Posts from real moments, not tips or frameworks.
One voice across every channel a buyer touches.

pillars.heading: How you sell
bodies: Most outreach goes out and lands nowhere. No replies, no signal, no idea why.
Yours runs as a system. DMs go out, replies come back, calls get booked.
Every call teaches the next. The offer gets clearer. The words land.
items: 1,000 outreach touches over 45 days, run by a trained operator.
Every sales call reviewed, with the language pulled into the next week's content and DMs.
5 to 10 booked qualified meetings if you have a warm network. 3 to 5 if you're fully cold.

stages.heading: One offer, everywhere.
bodies: We listen to your sales calls and look at your past clients. In those conversations is where we find the offer.
Once we pull it out, every surface a buyer reads gets rebuilt around it. Profile, site, posts, DMs.
stages.youLine: send recordings, share what you know about your clients, show up to one workshop and sign off the offer.
stages.meLine: prep the workshop, run it, and rebuild every surface around the offer we land on.

stages.heading: The system runs.
bodies: We turn it on. Outreach goes out daily. Content gets published every 3-4 days. Replies come back, and the first meetings hit your calendar.
Every call gets reviewed. What the buyer said goes back into next week's outreach.
stages.youLine: take the qualified conversations the DMs surface.
stages.meLine: write the posts. The VA runs the DMs.

stages.heading: Booked meetings, qualified leads.
bodies: Three weeks of replies and calls have made the outreach closer to what your buyer responds to.
The meetings show up warmer. The objections that come up are the real ones. Budget, timing, fit.
stages.meLine: learn from every call. That's where the real work is now.

groups.heading: What you offer
items: One offer, named and written down.
Profile, site, posts, and DMs rebuilt around it.
A 45-day content plan, with the first 10 posts written in your voice.

groups.heading: What you say
items: 1,000 outreach touches over 45 days.
A trained operator running the DMs on your LinkedIn.
Outreach SOPs your operator can keep running.

groups.heading: How you sell
items: Sales call training so you walk in knowing how to run them.
Every call reviewed with you, so you know exactly where it broke.
A system that turns those calls into next week's content and outreach.

groups.heading: Working with me
items: Workshop to land the offer.
10 Loom reviews across the sprint, plus a 30-min calibration call every two weeks.
Direct access, Monday to Friday on Slack or WhatsApp.

s6.headlineLine1: Rotterdam wasn't rebuilt in a day.
s6.bodyLine1: Sixty-plus coaches and consultants have been through some version of this work with me, positioning workshops, offer design, sales calls training, partial sprints.
s6.bodyLine2: Journify is the version that puts it all together.

faq.q: How much of my time does this actually take?
a: Week 1 is the heavy week. One workshop to land the offer, plus sending me your sales call recordings and what you know about your past clients.
After that, two to three hours a week. You review what I send, take the qualified conversations the DMs surface, and show up to the calibration calls.
By the time meetings start booking, you're just taking the meetings.
Solo or small-team service businesses, 1 to 10 people. You close through sales calls. You charge enough that one or two new clients a quarter changes the year.
Coaches. Consultants. Agencies. Founder-led service businesses.
If your buyer pays you €1,500 a month or more, you're in the right place.

faq.q: Who runs the outreach on my LinkedIn?
a: A trained operator working under my review. Your LinkedIn, your account, your name. Nothing happens that you don't see first.

faq.q: What if I don't get the meetings you promised?
a: 5 booked meetings if you have a warm network. 3 if you're cold.
If we don't hit that by day 45, I keep going. Outreach keeps running. I keep writing the posts. We keep reviewing the sales calls until the meetings show up.
Everything we built is yours. The offer. The rewritten profile and site. The content plan. The outreach SOPs. The trained operator.
If you want to keep the system running with me, there's a path for that. We talk about it on the call.

s8.payoff: Two active sprints at a time. Three pilot slots total.
